retry_reason: kref_get_unless_zero fails only when the orig_node refcount is already zero (object being freed); retrying will not revive the object.
contract_summary: This function looks up the global translation table entry for a given address and returns the best originator node (struct batadv_orig_node *) with an acquired reference. The caller must release this reference via batadv_orig_node_put() when done. The function also handles AP isolation by optionally searching the local table and checking isolation flags. On success, the returned orig_node is guaranteed to have a positive refcount; on failure (e.g., no global entry, AP isolated, or originator being freed), NULL is returned. Temporary references to local and global TT entries are properly released inside the function.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to search the translation table and return an originator node with an acquired reference; refcount management (get on orig_node, put on intermediate TT entries) is central, but includes AP isolation check and best-originator selection logic.

check.reason: kref_get_unless_zero is called on &orig_node->refcount, which increments the refcount of the returned orig_node. The access path from the return variable orig_node to the atomic_t field is refcount.refcount.refs, matching the type chain batadv_orig_node->kref->refcount_t->atomic_t. This is consistent with the callee's definition.
